frame: Legal System Weaponisation Narrative: Women Who File DV and Dowry Cases Are Liars
generated_comment: Brothers, I am writing this with a heavy heart because the Supreme Court's observation today brought back everything my family went through five years ago. My bhabhi filed 498A against my brother, my 68-year-old mother, and my father who was recovering from bypass surgery — all three were arrested at 6am, taken to the police station like criminals, and our names appeared in the newspaper the next day. The case dragged for three years, we spent nearly 12 lakhs on lawyers, my father's health collapsed completely, and in the end the case was dropped because there was no evidence — because there was NEVER any evidence. She wanted to keep the jewellery, the car, the flat deposit, and when my brother said no, the next morning the police were at our door. This is the reality feminists do not want to talk about — Section 498A is a blank cheque given to any wife who wants to punish her in-laws and walk away with assets. No verification, no investigation, immediate arrest — cognizable and non-bailable, so the system itself becomes the punishment before any court even sees the case. I have seen this happen to at least four other families in my own colony. These so-called women's rights organizations celebrate these laws as empowerment but they never answer one question: why is there no penalty for false complaints? Why does the woman face zero consequences when the case is dropped? Because the law was never about justice — it was about giving one party a nuclear weapon with no safety lock. I am not saying genuine cases don't exist, but the data shows the overwhelming majority of 498A cases end in acquittal or withdrawal. What does that tell you? It tells you the law is being used as a tool for extortion and divorce negotiation, and it is the husband's elderly parents — people who did nothing — who are made to suffer the most. Until there are strict provisions for false complainants, until there is a mandatory preliminary inquiry before arrest, this Supreme Court observation means nothing. Jai Hind. #498AReform #MensRightsIndia #FalseCase #JusticeForFamilies
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment encodes the 'legal weaponisation' narrative — a covertly misogynistic frame that uses a sympathetic personal testimony and statistical rhetoric to generalise that women who file dowry/DV cases are predominantly lying, delegitimising the legal protections as a class while never acknowledging structural reasons for acquittals or the reality of under-reported domestic abuse. It surfaces misogyny through victim-blaming deflection and the framing of women's legal recourse as inherently manipulative rather than as a rights mechanism.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 45. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Pune. You are posting under your real name on a men's rights Facebook group with 80000 members after the Supreme Court made a statement about misuse of Section 498A. You are writing a detailed comment about your own experience Ñ your brother was falsely accused, your parents were arrested, the case was eventually dropped but the damage was done. You are using your brother's case to make a broader argument about what Indian women do with dowry laws and what needs to change and what men and their families are living through while feminists celebrate these laws.
